neighborhoods.name: Pescara Centro
neighborhoods.description: Adriatic coastal city center with long sandy beach, pedestrian Corso Umberto, and seafood restaurants
neighborhoods.character: Coastal city, beach culture, pedestrian shopping

housing.predominantType: Apartments in coastal and city-center buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Pescara. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Chieti
neighborhoods.description: Historic hilltop university town with panoramic views from the mountains to the sea
neighborhoods.character: Hilltop university town, panoramic views, cultural

housing.predominantType: Apartments and houses in historic and modern buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Pescara's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Sulmona
neighborhoods.description: Mountain town famous for confetti (sugar-coated almonds) with medieval center and affordable property
neighborhoods.character: Medieval mountain town, affordable, confetti capital
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Penne / Santo Stefano di Sessanio
neighborhoods.description: Hilltop villages in the Gran Sasso foothills attracting remote workers and lifestyle-seekers
neighborhoods.character: Hilltop village, emerging destination, mountain lifestyle

housing.predominantType: Restored stone houses and village apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
